---
title: "All the World's a Stage"
author: "Rayne Aurit"
date: "2026-06-22"
categories: [Art]
image: "AllTheWorldsAStage.jpg"
---

![](AllTheWorldsAStage.jpg)
*Digital art; Rayne Aurit, May, 2025*

In the 2025 spring semester, I took the course Intro to Queer Literature as a breadth requirement of the College of Engineering. As an openly queer person, it was so fun to enjoy literature and engage in discussions with other people interested in topics that I am. Often, queer art, namely poetry, tends to be about capturing negative concepts and feelings. It is a natural thing. People gravitate towards metaphorical car wrecks as opposed to the simple joys that others experience. Tortured poets, starving artists, negative ideals fuel the art community with inspiration. Instead, I wanted to depict something more happy. 

Rates of suicide are high in the LGBTQ+ community, and among this political firestorm which centers transgender individuals, the mental health of the community is at a low. Yet, there are many queer people who have made it to old age and were pioneers before us. I captured this progression and the hope of making it to old age by colorizing my own interpretation of Shakespeare's "All the World's a Stage" monologue in the As You Like It comedy. Alongside the artwork, I wrote poems that indicate the stages of life that a queer person can, and often does, go through.

Presentation link: [https://canva.link/ce0alcofcqlfhg7](https://canva.link/ce0alcofcqlfhg7)

# Related Poetry

## All the World's a Machine

The world is not a stage

It’s a machine.

I wound up\
Ground up\
Spread within gears\
My entrails are not glitz nor glam\
Nor the rainbow that I don as a brilliant cape\

Teeth bite and graze\
To hone and destroy the\
Parts\
Of me that are out of place\
Take the saw to my rounded baby face\
Chisel in me the butch you see,\

The butch I want to become.

## All the World's a Playground

The world is not a stage

It is a playground.

I fell of the monkey bars yesterday\
I cannot be a monkey they say\

So I make my barbies into my monkeys\
And I brush their hair and make them do flips\

They’re really good at flips\
One day\
I will be able to do flips like I make them\

Then I make them kiss each other\
In the way friends do\

Friends do it at the playground, you see\
Barbies kissing other Barbies- well- because\

They don’t like Kens.

## All the World's a Book

The world is not a stage

It is a book.

The more I read, the more I want to read\
Until I have exhausted what I wish to exhaust\
And all the meaning is either lost or found\

My studies have a hold on me\
A much greater hold than\
My call home\

What is home?\
Is it where I go?\
Is it what I do?\
Is it the people I meet or the feelings of\
Blue.\

I admit I have lost more than I found

While the construction of my life began\
Things familiar had to be broken down\
Analyzed\
With the scrutiny I take to my own works\

Words create sentences\
Sentences create paragraphs\
Paragraphs create chapters\
Chapters create books\
Books create worlds\
Worlds to lose myself in\

I suppose I was always looking to be lost.

To lose oneself is the greatest way of finding oneself,\
Nestled between the words butch and non-binary\

Books break down into chapters\
Chapters break down into paragraphs\
Paragraphs break down into sentences\
Sentences break down into words\
Words break down into letters\

Letters are symbols that we assign meetings

Butch means me\
Lipstick means you\

Butch is short for butcher and the cleaver which\
I swing at all that aligns me with “woman”\

Lipstick is the thing you wear\
And leave all over the collars of my button-downs\

## All the World's a Lie

The world is not a stage

It is a lie.

The lie crept in overnight\
Slithering between bed sheets\

Discarded clothes--\
Maia was her name\

As sweet as it left my lips\
As sweet as she kissed my lips\

The first lesbian flag I held in my room--\
Gifted by her\

Girlfriend is an odd word--\
I wish it rolled off the tongue like boyfriend\

Too many consonants\
R then L then F then R again\

RLFR\
urlfur\

Play with it enough and you will get an earful

I think I felt true joy for the first time\
When I was with her\
I know I felt true anger when she left\

Oh, but the greatest fool between the two of us was me.

I, the fool

I, the follower

I, the frenzied butch bitch

Lifting weights to build muscle\
Did not give me the strength I needed\
When my world felt like it was caving in.\

The standards which you\
Held me to\
Suffocated the feminine traits out of me\

I still call myself a woman, you know.

## All the World's a Game

The world is not a stage

It is a game.

Monopoly where you play to the capitalist’s dream\
Life where you fill your car with pink and blue\
UNO where you demolish your friends with rules you made up\
Truth or dare where you bait others into crazy shit\
Risk where you simulate world domination\
Sorry where you never really apologize\
Never have I ever where nobodies win\
Cards against humanity where nobody wins\

I hold my cards close to my chest when I play\
This game\

This game throws out the needy\
Upholds the greedy\
Silences the quiet\
Treasures the loud\
Kicks out the homeless\
Encourages the crowd\
Abandons the depressed\
Romanticizes the struggle\

Stifles love\
Projects hate\
Burns the world\
While told to celebrate\
Things meant to kill\
Sold as measures of defense\
It will happen to them and not me\
I will protect them and not thee\

Is it still a game when the stakes are you and I?\
Is it still a game when the only options are do or die?\
A game to the politicians who push and pull for power\
A game to the capitalists who hate to see natural flowers\

At one point of my life\
The only great divide I knew\
Laid in Rocky Mountain National Park\
The continental divide\

Instead we get the informational divide\
While some try to turn a blind eye\

Red vs. blue\
Me vs. you\

## All the World's a House

The world is not a stage

It is a house.

At the end of the day\
As our pitchforks rest and we retire\
We gather about the hearth and wish\
For the other to get a good night’s sleep\

Or not.\
You do you bestie boo.\

Humanity’s greatest container is the house\
Though my mom tries convincing me\
Tupperware is a true life saver-- she’s right\

Then you broaden Tupperware to the fridge\
Then kitchen\
Then first floor\
And then you arrive back at house containing them all\

If you broaden it more, you get neighborhood\
Town\
County\
State\
Country\
Continent\
Then alas, you once again reach the world,\
and the World is not just the World.\

There is little meaning to draw behind something being itself

Soon spoken\
Soon retracted\

There is a certain quality extracted from the observation\
Of something as it is\
Delving into the meaning of something\
Pushes and pulls at its finite edges\

My views of the house changes the house\
For me\

The house is a haunting place.\
It reminds me of childish wishes, anger, walls enclosing\
The religious paraphernalia\
Glinting and glaring\
Taunting me for what I believe in no longer.\

The world houses what lies in the worst of humanity\
But a house is a house\
It is where I sleep\
It is made up of wood and hammered together with nails\
When the light shines through the window, I see\
My sister laughing\
My partner working\

Simplicity lives in the little things\
Their next door neighbor is Joy\

## All the World's a Meadow

The world is not a stage

It is a meadow.

The meadow which I return to\
And reside as the days go by\
You at my side\

We watch hand-in-hand\
The seeds we planted\
Blossom into the lavender-kissed gardens\

Pattie Gonia says that instead of\
Well-oiled machines\
We work together like a blossoming meadow\

The mycelium which holds us together\
The trees which keep out the elements\
The squirrels which prance with joy through the grass\

The birds which sing the song of the day\
The tulips which gently sway\
With the wind and then bloom just on time\

Choose which bundle of life you are
